text: [02] Timeline & DOOM Clock // These events are what happens if the party does not act. TIMELINE 1850 -The region’s largest mine opens in Carpenter, OR. 1893 -A rash of deaths in the mine makes it unprosperous. 1933 - Carpenter is hit hard by drought and The Great Depression. Continued disappearances feed into local legends. 1937 - Carpenter is chosen as one of the sites of The New Deal, bringing jobs and reinvestment to the area. 1960 -The mall is built atop the old mine. It becomes a thriving pillar of the community. 1987 -The mall experiences its first big-box closure. 1989 -Mr. Neil(p. 6) is hired to manage the mall. DOOM CLOCK DOOM Clock (1-10): A measure on how close the party is to DOOM. Actions taken by the PCs change the progression (and details) of the DOOM clock. DOOM 1 -It’s 7pm! Visitors gather at Contest Stage (p. 12) for the raffle and speeches. DOOM 2 - The mall fully phases out of reality. A Child of Ammon (p. 9) emerges, killing at least one NPC. DOOM 3 -People quickly realize a Barrier (p. 12) has trapped them in the mall. DOOM 4 -Factions (p. 6) emerge and begin to amass resources. DOOM 5 -A Child of Ammon (p. 9) is caught mid-gestation, inside of a victim. DOOM 6 -Power goes out. Water supply becomes finite. DOOM 7 - The REPLACED (p. 8) begin sabotaging the mall’s supplies. The fleshy mass- of-pets will escape the pet shop. DOOM 8 -Water is sabotaged and ceases to flow from plumbing. DOOM 9 - Fungal Fields (p. 26) appear throughout the mall, sprouting from spores and the dead. DOOM 10 - With enough bodies, the Portal (p. 27) becomes a breach and the mall phases back to reality. SUMMER 1999 1999 - Last month, Mr. Neil(p. 6) began kidnapping and sacrificing people. He used some to build a portal in the minesbelow the mall’s Bunker (p. 23). He turned the rest into Fruiting Bodies (p. 13). They’re in the vents now, shedding spores to infect others. 1999 - Last week, Mr. Neil brought forth a Child of Ammon (p. 9), a piece of a creature from beyond the portal. It is gestating inside of him. 1999 - Today, the community is attempting to save the failing mall by raffling off an Oldsmobile. This module starts at DOOM 1. Timeline Credits & Table of Contents // Those who helped create this modern horror dungeon. [03] THE MALL - REMASTERED Writing, Layout & Design: Goblin Archives © 2024 Editing: Vi Huntsman Proofreading: Jarrett Crader Cover: Zach Hazard Vaupen © 2022 Illustrations: Garbage Goat (p. 11, 12, 13, 15, 17, 18, 20) © 2022 Illustration: Trevor Henderson (p. 10) © 2022 Additional Cartography/Graphic: Luke Saunders (p. 21-27) © 2024 Playtesters: Chris, Will, Geoff, Andy, Paul, Tyler, Nick, Shadow and John Consulting: Chrissy Crits, Will Conwell, Garbage Goat, Logan Dean, Josh Domanski. You can use your own mall as the setting too. MAIN ENTRANCE DMV POLLY’S PET EMPORIUM BUSINESS ASSOCIATION EMMY’S SWORDS SKATE WORLD MAINTENANCE LOADING DOCKS FREIGHT ELEVATOR YOUR BODY IS A TEMPLE WELESS CLINIC SECURITY FOOD COURT HELP DESK STAN’S PRETZELS MANAGER’S OFFICE JOE’S BURGER SPIRAL CINEMA BREAK ROOM MOOTSY’S ARCADE STAGE PARKING GARAGE 1 PARKING GARAGE 1 PARKING GARAGE 2 PARKING GARAGE 2 PARKING GARAGE 3 PARKING GARAGE 3 PARKING GARAGE 3 LEVEL 1 LEVEL 2 LEVEL 3 PARKING GARAGE 1 THE MALL: VISITORS MAP Directory & Reference // Players create additional stores and NPCs at character creation. [05] STORE DIRECTORY PERSONNEL 1. Mr. Neil - Mall Manager (p. 6) 2. Billy - Reports directly to Mr. Neil (p. 6) 3. Kurt - Stan’s Pretzel Stand (p. 8) 4. Maggie - Head of the CBB (p. 6) 5. Griffith -Your Body is a Temple (p. 7) 6. Samuel - Griffith’s right hand (p. 7) 7. William- Head mall walker (p. 6) 8. Bruce - Maintenance (p. 8) 9. 10. 11. Beth - Head of the Mall Rats (p. 7) 12. 13. 14. Ruth - Patron (p. 7) Emmy - Emmy’s Swords & Stuff (p. 8) Logan - DMV Express (p. 8) RULES & REFERENCE Liminal Horror Rules: Annotations for core rules are noted as (LH p. xx). PDF is hyperlinked. Character Creation(p. 32): Custom Mall- specific Character Creation procedures. Custom Fallouts(p. 30): Mall-specific Fallouts, to be used in place of, or in addition to, the core Fallouts. Whispers Cards(p. 37): Secret prompts triggered by character action (including every Fallout and often replacing Stress). A few cards cause a PC to become REPLACED. Nightmare & Prophecies(p. 31): To sleep is to dream. All PCs make CTRL saves when sleeping. Failing means rolling on the Nightmare Table. Have They Been REPLACED?(p. 8): NPCs may be REPLACED, roll a d10 against the DOOM level. If the result is equal or under AND they fail a CTRL save, they have been REPLACED. As the adventure progresses, NPCs’ CTRL may decrease. Mall Size: 1,472,000 sq ft, 178 mostly-vacant shop fronts, 3 floors, domed skylight, 3 garages. Number of NPCs: Approximately 100 visitors and business owners. Utilities: Electricity via generators (backup fuel in the basement). Water via cistern and pump. Vents: Ventilation shafts web through the mall, connecting rooms and levels. Level 1 1. Contest Stage (p. 12) 2. Outside the Mall (p. 12) 3. Parking Garages - all levels (p. 13) 4. Vents - all levels (p. 13) 5. Maintenance (p. 14) 6. Loading Docks (p. 14) 7. Your Body is a Temple (p. 14) 8. Polly’s
